s2.q: What is the definition of compound presentation?
options: Cephalic presentation with a prolapsed limb beside the presenting part
s2.explain: Compound presentation is a cephalic presentation in which a limb (usually upper limb, less commonly lower limb) prolapses beside the presenting part.

s2.q: When assessing this aspect, the most important factor is:
s2.explain: Assessment requires integration of clinical, laboratory, and imaging findings.

s2.q: The incidence of this condition is highest in which population?
options: Depends on the specific condition and risk factors
s2.explain: Incidence varies by condition — each has its own epidemiology and risk profile.

s2.q: The clinical significance of this section is:
options: It applies only to research settings
s2.explain: Understanding these concepts directly informs clinical management.

s2.q: In obstetric practice, this knowledge helps:
s2.explain: Comprehensive knowledge enables prevention, early diagnosis, and optimal management.

s3.q: Which of the following is a cause of compound presentation?
s3.explain: Prematurity is a cause of compound presentation. Other causes include PROM, polyhydramnios, multiparity, contracted pelvis, pelvic tumors, and twins.

s3.q: How many etiologic factors for compound presentation does the source list?
s3.explain: The source lists 7 etiologic factors: prematurity, PROM, polyhydramnios, multiparity, contracted pelvis, pelvic tumors, and twins — all create extra room at the pelvic inlet.

s3.explain: Comprehensive knowledge enables prevention, early diagnosis, and optimal management.

s4.q: How is compound presentation definitively diagnosed?
options: Vaginal examination showing prolapsed limb beside the presenting part
s4.explain: Compound presentation is definitively diagnosed on PV examination when the prolapsed limb is felt beside the presenting part.

s4.q: Which clinical finding is most suggestive of this diagnosis?
options: Varies by condition — specific findings guide diagnosis
s4.explain: Clinical presentation differs by condition and guides diagnostic testing.

s5.q: What is the conservative management for compound presentation?
options: Gently reduce the prolapsed limb and push the head down
s5.explain: The conservative step is to gently try to reduce the prolapsed limb upward above the pelvic brim and hold it till contraction, then push the head down into the pelvis.

s5.q: Which of the following is an indication for cesarean section in compound presentation?
s5.explain: CS is indicated in compound presentation if any of the following develops: abnormal FHR pattern, cord prolapse, or poor progress of labor.
